name: Priority One
description_short: Member-owned credit union offering checking, auto loans, personal loans, credit cards, and home loans since 1926. Emphasizes low fees, local service, and competitive rates.
description_long:
Priority One Credit Union has been operating since 1926, approaching its 100th anniversary in 2026. As a member-owned financial institution, the organization positions itself as a community-focused alternative to traditional banks, with an emphasis on personalized service and member benefits. The credit union operates a modern digital banking platform while maintaining local branch service.

Priority One offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including checking and savings accounts, auto loans (including refinancing), motorcycle and boat loans, personal loans, credit cards, home loans with equity options, and tax loans. Their current advertised rates include credit cards as low as 13.50% APR, new auto loans from 5.75% APR, motorcycle loans from 7.89% APR, and boat loans from 9.74% APR. They also promote auto loan refinancing savings of up to 2% and tax loans up to $5,000 with 24-month terms.

The credit union distinguishes itself through member ownership structure, claiming lower fees than competitors, emphasis on local community service, and financial education resources. Marketing highlights personalized support from staff familiar with local communities, and member testimonials reference positive customer service experiences. The organization promotes itself as putting member needs first with a century of financial experience.

As a credit union, Priority One serves member-owners rather than shareholders, which theoretically aligns incentives toward member benefit. However, membership requirements and eligibility criteria are not disclosed on the website. While competitive rates are advertised, actual approval rates, credit score requirements, and terms for various products remain unstated. The website does not provide transparent information about membership fees, minimum deposits, or eligibility restrictions that may apply.
services: Auto loans (new and refinancing) up to 100% LTV
Personal loans for consolidation and unexpected expenses
Home loans including home equity loans and lines of credit
Tax loans up to $5,000 with 24-month terms
Member financial counseling and support services
pros: Member-owned credit union structure potentially offering better alignment with customer interests than shareholder-owned banks
Competitive auto loan rates starting at 5.75% APR with options for 100% financing including taxes and fees
Diverse loan products including tax loans up to $5,000 with flexible 24-month terms
Advertised low-fee structure compared to traditional banks
Digital banking platform with security features plus local branch service availability
Over 100 years of operating history (since 1926) providing institutional stability
Youth savings products available (Youth Share Certificate)
Auto loan refinancing options with advertised savings up to 2%
cons: Website does not disclose membership eligibility requirements, fees, or minimum deposit amounts
No transparent information about credit score requirements or loan approval criteria
Actual APR rates shown are minimums; full rate ranges and qualification factors not disclosed
Limited detail on home loan programs despite prominently featuring them
No information about account fees, overdraft policies, or other standard banking costs
best_for: Members seeking auto financing or refinancing with competitive rates and flexible terms
Community members who prefer local credit union service over large national banks
Borrowers needing personal loans or tax anticipation loans with flexible repayment terms
Families interested in youth savings products and financial education resources
diagnosis: Priority One is best suited for individuals already eligible for credit union membership who prefer community-based banking with competitive rates on auto, personal, and home loans. The main caveat is that the website provides minimal transparency on membership requirements, actual fee structures, and specific qualification criteria—essential information needed to fully assess value compared to traditional banks.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Priority One — South Pasadena, CA Credit Union | CreditDoc
meta_description: Priority One — NCUA-insured credit union in South Pasadena, CA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
